title: notocactus oxycostatus subsp. gracilis
intro: Notocactus oxycostatus subsp. gracilis, commonly known as the Gracilis Hedgehog Cactus, is a rare, subspecies of cactus found in Brazil, characterized by its short, thick stems covered with sharp, yellowish spines. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Notocactus oxycostatus subsp. gracilis include relief from coughs, congestion, and inflammation, making it a popular choice in traditional herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
